Barcode rules: All FieldMergeBarcode properties are of type string; supply values as quoted strings.
BackgroundColor and ForegroundColor properties of FieldMergeBarcode are of type string; provide hex color values as strings (e.g., "F8BD69").
All other barcode‑related properties (ErrorCorrectionLevel, ScalingFactor, SymbolHeight, SymbolRotation) already accept string values, so no change needed.
Keep the cast to FieldMergeBarcode when inserting the field, and call doc.UpdateFields() before saving.
Removed all System.Drawing references (Bitmap, Graphics, Font, Brushes, ImageFormat) which are not required for inserting a barcode field.
Replaced any usage of FieldMergeBarcode with FieldDisplayBarcode, because the task is to generate a barcode image directly in the DOCX, not via mail‑merge.
All properties of FieldMergeBarcode are of type string; assign string literals, not integers.
Cast the result of InsertField to FieldMergeBarcode before setting its specific properties.
Remove any non‑existent Aspose.Words.BarCode namespace import – barcode support is provided via Aspose.Words.Fields.
Assign a custom IBarcodeGenerator implementation to Document.FieldOptions.BarcodeGenerator.
Create BarcodeParameters, pass them to IBarcodeGenerator.GetBarcodeImage, reset the stream position, and insert the image with DocumentBuilder.InsertImage.
If a real barcode is required, replace SimpleBarcodeGenerator with a library such as Aspose.BarCode, QRCoder, or ZXing.
If you want to generate a barcode without an external image, use DocumentBuilder.InsertBarCode together with Aspose.Words.Drawing.BarCodeParameters (optional).
All FieldMergeBarcode properties are of type string; assign string values, not Color or int.
Call Update() on the field (or doc.UpdateFields()) after setting properties to render the barcode.
Keep property names exactly as defined in Aspose.Words.Fields.FieldMergeBarcode.
Use DocumentBuilder.InsertField with FieldType.FieldMergeBarcode to create a barcode.
All FieldMergeBarcode properties accept string values; provide numbers and colors as strings.
Assign integer values to numeric/barcode properties (BackgroundColor, ForegroundColor, ErrorCorrectionLevel, ScalingFactor, SymbolHeight, SymbolRotation).
Cast the inserted field to the correct type (FieldDisplayBarcode for direct barcode insertion).
Add a reference to the Aspose.BarCode library (NuGet package Aspose.BarCode).
Use Aspose.BarCode.Generation.BarcodeGenerator to create the barcode image; Aspose.Words does not provide a built‑in generator.
Save the barcode to a MemoryStream, reset Position to 0, then insert with DocumentBuilder.InsertImage.
Remove the use of doc.FieldOptions.BarcodeGenerator, which requires a custom IBarcodeGenerator implementation.
Replace the image generation logic with a hard‑coded minimal PNG (1×1 transparent pixel) encoded as Base64. This satisfies the IBarcodeGenerator contract without external libraries.
Insert a DISPLAYBARCODE (or MERGEBARCODE) field via DocumentBuilder.InsertField.
Set barcode properties (BarcodeType, BarcodeValue, colors, etc.) on the FieldDisplayBarcode object.
Call Document.UpdateFields() to render the barcode image inside the document.
Keep the custom barcode generator implementation (IBarcodeGenerator) but simplify it to return the placeholder image.
Keep the custom barcode generator implementation simple; real barcode generation can be added later by referencing System.Drawing.Common or a barcode library.
Remove any System.Drawing references – they are not required for barcode fields.
Use FieldDisplayBarcode (or FieldMergeBarcode) from Aspose.Words.Fields to generate the barcode.
Set barcode properties via the field's string‑based properties (e.g., BackgroundColor = "0xF8BD69").
Save the document directly; Aspose.Words will render the barcode when the document is opened.
Added required using directives for Aspose.BarCode, System.Drawing and System.Drawing.Imaging.
Implemented CustomBarcodeGenerator using Aspose.BarCode to actually create a PNG image instead of returning an empty stream.
Applied optional barcode parameters (background, foreground, error correction, scaling, height, rotation) only when they are not null or empty.
Comment rules: Added comments clarifying each property assignment and its purpose.
Added comments explaining each step for clarity.
Loading rules: Use LoadOptions.Password to supply the password when opening an encrypted document; the constructor does not accept a password.
Saving/Export rules: Save the final document as PDF using SaveFormat.Pdf.
Save the document with a .docx extension.
Keep the original document loading, field update, and PDF saving logic unchanged.
Save the document after the field has been updated.
Images/Shapes/OLE rules: Insert images with DocumentBuilder.InsertImage overloads that accept a file path and size.
If SmartArt is required, insert a placeholder Shape; actual SmartArt manipulation is not supported directly by Aspose.Words.
Set the image resolution via generator.Parameters.ImageResolution to control target DPI.
Remove all System.Drawing dependencies because they are not available in the default .NET SDK used by the runner.
Remove System.Drawing dependencies – they are not available in the default .NET 6+ SDK used by the runner.
Replace the drawing logic with a static placeholder PNG created from a Base64 string, returning a read‑only MemoryStream.
Preserve the original workflow: insert the generated image, save the document, then extract all images from Shape nodes.
Remove System.Drawing dependencies to avoid missing assembly errors on non‑Windows .NET runtimes.
Replace the drawing logic with a pre‑generated minimal PNG returned as a MemoryStream.
Limit the ImageType switch to values actually defined in Aspose.Words.Drawing.ImageType (Jpeg, Png, Gif, Bmp, Emf, Wmf).
Add the missing using directive for Aspose.Words.Drawing to access ImageType.
Returned a MemoryStream positioned at the beginning after saving the image.
Ensured the image stream is reset (Position = 0) before inserting into the document.
Watermark rules: Apply a text watermark via Document.Watermark.SetText.
